special_effects.description: In combat, when one side achieves more levels of success than the other in a Differential Roll, they gain Special Effects equal to the difference. Effects are chosen from the table below.

table.name: Accidental Injury
table.description: Defender deflects/twists attacker's strike so they injure themselves. Attacker rolls own weapon damage on random hit location. If unarmed, tears/breaks something internal (ignores armour).

table.name: Arise
table.description: Defender uses momentary opening to roll back to their feet.

table.name: Bash
table.description: Knock opponent off balance. Shields: 1m per 2 damage rolled. Bludgeoning: 1m per 3 damage. Works on creatures up to 2x attacker's SIZ. If forced into obstacle: Hard Athletics/Acrobatics or fall.

table.name: Bleed
table.description: Cut open major blood vessel. Must overcome AP and injure. Defender: opposed Endurance vs original attack. Failure: lose 1 Fatigue level per Combat Round until collapse/death. Staunched by First Aid; violent action re-opens wound.

table.name: Blind Opponent
table.description: Briefly blind attacker (sand, reflected light, etc.). Attacker: opposed Evade (or weapon style if using shield) vs defender's parry roll. Failure: Blindness modifier for 1d3 turns.

table.name: Bypass Armour
table.description: Find gap in natural or worn armour. If wearing armour above natural, must choose which to bypass. Can stack to bypass both. Magic protection counts as worn.

table.name: Choose Location
table.description: Freely select hit location for melee (must be within reach). Ranged: Critical only, unless target is stationary/unaware at close range.

table.name: Circumvent Cover
table.description: Fire around target's cover (self-guided ammo or trick shot). Trick shots treated as Critical only with reduced damage.

table.name: Circumvent Parry
table.description: Completely bypass an otherwise successful parry.

table.name: Close Range
table.description: Automatically change engagement range to favour shorter weapon.

table.name: Compel Surrender
table.description: Force surrender of helpless/disadvantaged opponent (disarmed, prone, serious wound, etc.). No damage dealt — only threatened. Opposed Willpower vs original attack/parry roll. GM may reserve for NPCs only.

table.name: Damage Weapon
table.description: Damage opponent's weapon. Apply damage to weapon's AP/HP instead of wielder. Weapon at 0 HP = broken.

table.name: Disarm Opponent
table.description: Knock/yank/twist weapon from hand. Opposed Combat Style vs original roll. Weapon size difference adjusts difficulty (larger weapon = easier disarm). Weapon flung Damage Modifier metres. Works up to 2x attacker's STR.

table.name: Drop Foe
table.description: Target must pass Opposed Endurance vs attack roll or become incapacitated from shock/pain. Recovery via First Aid, magic, or wait (1 hour / Healing Rate).

table.name: Duck Back
table.description: Immediately duck back into cover without waiting for next Turn. Must be adjacent to cover.

table.name: Enhance Parry
table.description: Deflect entire force of attack regardless of weapon Size.

table.name: Entangle
table.description: Immobilize struck location (arm: can't use item; leg: can't move; head/chest/abdomen: all skills one grade harder). Next turn: auto Trip Opponent attempt for 1 AP. Victim can break free via Brawn opposed roll, or win SE for Damage Weapon/Disarm/Slip Free.

table.name: Flurry
table.description: Immediate follow-up unarmed attack with different limb/body part. Costs 1 AP. Allows several attacks in sequence before defender responds offensively.

table.name: Force Failure
table.description: Combine with any SE requiring an opposed roll — opponent automatically fails resistance. E.g., Force Failure + Disarm = auto-disarm.

table.name: Grip
table.description: Hold onto opponent with empty hand/claw/tail/tentacle within Unarmed reach. Prevents changing weapon range or disengaging. Break free: opposed Brawn or Unarmed vs gripper's choice. Some grippers too strong to escape.

table.name: Impale
table.description: Roll weapon damage twice, choose better result. If armour penetrated and wound caused: option to leave weapon in wound (difficulty penalty based on creature SIZ vs weapon size) or yank free next turn (half normal damage, no DM, ignores armour). Impaled weapon can't parry.

table.name: Kill Silently
table.description: Requires Assassination combat style trait. Surprised targets only, first attack only. Cover mouth + stab/cut/garrotte. Prevents victim crying out for entire round. Serious/Major wound = auto-fail Endurance.

table.name: Marksman
table.description: Move hit location one step to adjacent body area. Must follow physiology.

table.name: Maximise Damage
table.description: Substitute one weapon damage die for its maximum value. E.g., 1d6 → 6, 2d6 → 1d6+6. Does not affect Damage Modifier (must be rolled normally). Works for natural weapons.

table.name: Open Range
table.description: Change engagement range to favour longer weapon.

table.name: Overextend Opponent
table.description: Defender sidesteps/retreats — opponent can't attack next turn.

table.name: Overpenetration
table.description: Shot passes through first target to hit second behind (if overcomes body armour). Second victim: half damage. Other SEs not applied to second target.

table.name: Pin Down
table.description: Opposed Willpower vs attack roll. Failure: target hunkers behind cover, can't return fire next Turn. Works even if no damage dealt (intimidation). Target can crawl, reload, communicate while pinned.

table.name: Pin Weapon
table.description: Pin opponent's weapon/shield using body/positioning. Opponent must spend AP and wrestle free (as per Grip). Failure = item stays unusable.

table.name: Prepare Counter
table.description: Pre-plan counter to specific SE (note secretly). If opponent uses that SE, auto-substitute with your own offensive or defensive SE.

table.name: Press Advantage
table.description: Pressure opponent to stay defensive — can't attack next turn. Establishes unbroken attack sequence. Foes will disengage, call for help, or use Prepare Counter.

table.name: Rapid Reload
table.description: Reduce reload time by 1.

table.name: Remise
table.description: Sequential follow-up attack with Small weapon on opponent's next turn. Forces foe to change proactive action to reactive.

table.name: Scar Foe
table.description: Permanent disfiguring scar (slice across face, inscribed letter, etc.).

table.name: Select Target
table.description: Deflect fumbled blow into adjacent bystander. New target must be within reach (melee) or line of fire (ranged). Auto-hit, no SE. Target completely surprised.

table.name: Slip Free
table.description: Auto-escape Entangle, Grip, or Pin.

table.name: Spoil Spell
table.description: Automatically ruin spell being cast, if blow overcomes AP and injures target.

table.name: Stand Fast
table.description: Brace against knockback effects of any damage received.

table.name: Stun Location
table.description: Must overcome AP and injure. Opposed Endurance vs original attack. Failure: location incapacitated for turns = damage dealt. Torso: stagger, defend only. Head: briefly insensible.

table.name: Sunder
table.description: Damage armour AP value. Weapon damage (after reductions for parry/magic) applied to armour's AP. Surplus AP damage reduces armour permanently. Remaining damage after AP reaches 0 carries to location HP.

table.name: Take Weapon
table.description: Yank/twist weapon from opponent's hand. Opposed Combat Style vs original Unarmed roll. Loser's weapon is taken and usable by winner. Size irrelevant, but only works on creatures up to 2x attacker's STR.

table.name: Trip Opponent
table.description: Overbalance/throw opponent to ground. Opposed Brawn, Evade, or Acrobatics vs original roll. Quadrupeds may substitute Athletics and treat roll one grade easier.

table.name: Weapon Malfunction
table.description: Attacker's weapon malfunctions. Useless until repaired.

table.name: Withdraw
table.description: Auto-disengage from opponent. Break engagement without needing Change Range or Outmanoeuvre.

table.situation: Defending while on lower ground or against mounted foe
Fighting whilst crouching or from one knee
Fighting in poor visibility (thick fog, snowstorm)
Defending against an attack from behind
Blinded or loss of primary perceptive sense

close_combat_situational_modifiers.note: Applied to attacking, parrying, and evading equally unless stated otherwise. Use most severe if multiple situations apply.
